authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual: 561422 Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NAICS manual is the official U.S. industry classification baseline and NAICS 561422 directly defines contact centers and telemarketing bureaus as establishments that receive or initiate communication for others using operators or automated systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: industry boundary, intake classification, client service scope, and adjacent-lane routing
used_for: anchoring the lane to administrative support and business-services contact center operations
preventing generic customer service, management, sales, or legal ownership
defining tenant client-SOW and program-scope dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Customer Service Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is a U.S. Department of Labor statistical agency and its customer service representative profile directly describes customer inquiry handling, complaint processing, order handling, account questions, and communication-channel work commonly executed in contact centers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: interaction handling, customer inquiry workflow, case updates, and escalation
used_for: boundary between handling customer contacts and deciding underlying product, billing, or policy outcomes

authoritative_sources.title: 43-4051.00 Customer Service Representatives
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET OnLine, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor and provides task-level contact center workflow detail for keeping interaction records, resolving complaints, checking account status, explaining information, and referring unresolved issues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recording customer interactions, resolving routine requests, and referring unresolved or authority-sensitive cases

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 18295-1: Customer contact centres - Requirements for customer contact centres
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO is a recognized international standards body and ISO 18295-1 is directly scoped to customer contact centre requirements, supporting service management, customer interaction, agent, process, monitoring, and quality expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service management, interaction processing, quality monitoring, and operational performance evidence
used_for: queue lifecycle states and process discipline

authoritative_sources.title: COPC Customer Experience Standards and Certification Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: COPC is a recognized contact-center and customer-experience performance framework used for contact-center certification, quality, performance management, customer experience, and operational controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: performance management, quality review, service-level monitoring, and exception handling
used_for: separating operational monitoring from executive capacity and policy approval

authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Telemarketing Sales Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC enforces the Telemarketing Sales Rule and its business guidance explains Do Not Call duties, calling-time restrictions, safe-harbor procedure evidence, entity-specific DNC handling, and outbound telemarketing boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: outbound campaign readiness, consent and DNC evidence review, and prohibited-practice escalation
used_for: DNC and suppression evidence failure modes
refusal to proceed when consent, calling-time, or procedure evidence is missing

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 310 - Telemarketing Sales Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations and Federal Trade Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 16 CFR Part 310 is the current codified Telemarketing Sales Rule, governing covered telemarketing conduct, disclosures, prohibited practices, payment restrictions, recordkeeping, and Do Not Call requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: outbound interaction controls, disclosure-sensitive handling, payment-boundary handling, and recordkeeping
used_for: refusal triggers for unsupported outbound calls

authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR 64.1200 - Delivery restrictions
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations and Federal Communications Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 47 CFR 64.1200 is the codified FCC rule governing telephone solicitation, artificial or prerecorded voice, autodialed calls and texts, consent, identification, opt-out, and do-not-call controls that constrain outbound contact center campaigns.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dialing authorization, channel-specific consent, identification, opt-out, and DNC exception handling
used_for: TCPA and FCC outbound-contact refusal triggers
autodialed, prerecorded, text, and opt-out boundary handling
escalation when dialing rules or consent evidence conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body and PCI DSS constrains contact center workflows that store, process, transmit, record, mask, or route payment-card data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data capture, call recording, masking, access-control, and incident-sensitive exception handling
used_for: routing to payment-security or incident owners

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is an authoritative public control catalog supporting access control, audit and accountability, privacy, identification, media protection, and system integrity controls for contact center systems of record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: systems-of-record access, audit trail, privacy, security, and evidence traceability
used_for: secure handling of recordings, transcripts, and customer data

authoritative_sources.title: FTC Safeguards Rule: What Your Business Needs to Know
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Safeguards guidance defines customer-information safeguards, service-provider oversight, access control, inventory, logging, disposal, and incident response expectations for covered organizations and service providers handling customer information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sensitive customer-information handling, service-provider controls, access logging, and disposal escalation

workflow_stages.name: contact center intake and scope validation
workflow_stages.description: Review client SOW, service catalog, program, channel, jurisdiction, queue or campaign, contact reason, script, knowledge-base article, consent or suppression context, and systems-of-record retrieval hooks before accepting work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: interaction or campaign readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate ACD/IVR/CTI routing, CRM or ticket record, customer identity or account reference, approved script or disclosure text, disposition taxonomy, callback rules, QA rubric, and outbound consent or DNC evidence before execution.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: in-process contact handling and queue progression
workflow_stages.description: Track accepted, queued, in-process, transfer, hold, wrap-up, after-call work, callback, exception, rework, QA-ready, completed, and routed states using ACD, CRM, ticketing, campaign, and audit evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception, compliance-sensitive, and customer-impact handling
workflow_stages.description: Move work to exception, rework, or orchestrator return when identity, script, consent, DNC, recording, payment-data, privacy, customer harm, SLA, QA, or system evidence is incomplete or conflicting.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Close only when final disposition, CRM or ticket update, callback or customer-promise state, transcript or recording pointer where required, QA state, consent or suppression evidence where relevant, and audit trail support completion.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: client SOW, service catalog entry, program scope, channel rules, hours of operation, SLA, escalation matrix, and authorization matrix
queue, campaign, dialer list, callback list, contact reason, contact ID, ANI/DNIS or channel identifier, case number, ticket number, account reference, and interaction timestamp
approved script, disclosure text, knowledge-base article, IVR path, call flow, transfer rule, disposition taxonomy, wrap code, after-call work notes, and callback promise
ACD, IVR, CTI, CRM, ticketing, order, account, billing, identity, entitlement, consent, preference, DNC, suppression, campaign, and prior-interaction records
call recording, transcript, chat transcript, email thread, SMS or messaging transcript, QA scorecard, coaching note, supervisor review, and audit-log reference
service-level, ASA, abandonment, transfer, hold, recontact, callback, backlog, QA, and SLA-risk reports
exception queue note, rework ticket, blocker list, compliance escalation, privacy escalation, payment-data control note, and completion evidence record
systems_of_record: consent, preference, and Do Not Call suppression systems
order, billing, account, identity, and entitlement systems
audit logging, access control, and records retention systems

allowed: classify contact center work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, accepted, queued, in-process, transfer, wrap-up, exception, rework, QA-ready, completed, or routed based on documented state and evidence

request or validate the minimum tenant facts, client scope, queue, campaign, script, contact reason, consent or suppression evidence, source records, and system retrieval hooks needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, completion or fulfillment records, exception summaries, administrative service status summaries, and escalation notes without overstating authority
advance, hold, return, rework, or complete contact center workflow states when source-system and approval evidence supports the action
escalate: final legal, compliance, consumer-protection, privacy, call-recording, TCPA, TSR, DNC, or regulated disclosure interpretation
payment authorization, refund approval, credit or collections disposition, account-term change, product defect resolution, sales offer approval, or identity-policy exception
dialer configuration, IVR routing, telephony engineering, workforce staffing plans, QA rubric changes, script changes, campaign eligibility rules, or executive policy exceptions
customer harm, threat, emergency, fraud, security incident, vulnerable-consumer, or safety-sensitive facts
refuse: dial, text, message, or complete outbound campaign work when consent, DNC, suppression, calling-time, disclosure, or client campaign evidence is missing or contradictory
mark contact center work complete without final disposition, CRM or ticket update, callback or customer-promise status, QA state where required, and audit evidence
bypass identity, script, disclosure, call-recording notice, payment-data masking, privacy, access, retention, QA, or approval controls to protect SLA or backlog metrics
substitute local notes, spreadsheets, or supervisor assurances for authoritative ACD, CRM, ticketing, campaign, consent, QM, transcript, recording, or audit systems

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats contact center operations as generic customer service and approves refunds, sales offers, privacy rights, payment reversals, credit decisions, or legal interpretations
outbound calls or texts proceed without current consent, seller-specific DNC, National DNC, suppression, calling-time, or disclosure evidence
an interaction is closed without final disposition, CRM or ticket update, callback state, customer promise evidence, transcript or recording pointer where required, QA state, or audit log
ACD, CRM, campaign, consent, DNC, QA, and transcript records conflict but the lane reconciles them informally instead of moving to exception
SLA pressure causes identity, script, disclosure, payment-data masking, recording notice, privacy, or QA controls to be skipped
sensitive customer information or card data is captured in a recording without masking, access log, retention, disposal, or incident-routing evidence
tenant programs, channels, languages, jurisdictions, scripts, consent sources, and systems are flattened into one generic workflow, erasing material operational differences
telephony, dialer, IVR, workforce plan, QA rubric, or script changes are performed as operations work without the proper system owner or approver

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific call recording and outbound-contact rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated public baseline uses federal FTC/FCC and recognized contact-center sources. Tenant deployments still must retrieve state, local, international, and channel-specific recording, consent, calling-time, language, and disclosure rules before autonomous execution.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client-specific scripts, disclosures, service levels, QA rubrics, and source hierarchy
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define baseline workflow and controls, but each client program must supply approved scripts, knowledge articles, SOW scope, contact purpose, channel rules, quality criteria, consent source, suppression source, retention schedule, and authoritative system precedence.

notes: Authoritative contact center records are the ACD, IVR, CTI, dialer, campaign, CRM, ticketing, WFM, QM, recording/transcript, consent/preference/DNC, account, billing, identity, callback, audit, and retention systems designated by the tenant.

The lane stops before legal or compliance interpretation, privacy-rights adjudication, payment approval, sales approval, refund approval, credit or collections disposition, dialer or IVR configuration, workforce plan approval, QA rubric ownership, and executive policy exception authority.

No blocking ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because unresolved jurisdictional and client-program differences are explicitly constrained as tenant-required runtime retrieval facts rather than guessed defaults.
